content.problemStatement:
You are given a string `S`.

Your task is to check whether the **number of visible characters** (excluding leading and trailing spaces) is **greater than 5 or less than or equal to 5**.


If the visible length is greater than 5, print `"Long String"`.
Otherwise, print `"Short String"`.
**Be careful**!


Spaces at **the beginning and end** should not be counted.
Spaces **in the middle must be counted**.
The comparison should be **case-insensitive** (e.g., `CodeChef` and `codechef` are treated the same).

Input Format
The first and only line contains a string `S`.



Output Format
Print one of the following:



`"Long String"` — if visible length > 5
`"Short String"` — if visible length ≤ 5
constraints: S consists only of English letters (a‑z, A‑Z) and space characters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a string S which may have leading, trailing, and internal spaces.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case, output on a separate line:
"Long String" if the number of visible characters (after removing leading and trailing spaces) is greater than 5, otherwise "Short String".

sampleTestCases.explanation: After trimming, "CodeChef" has 8 characters (>5), so the output is "Long String".
"RCPIT" has exactly 5 characters, which is not greater than 5, so the output is "Short String".
Trimming removes the surrounding spaces, leaving "Hello World" (11 characters). Since 11 > 5, the output is "Long String".

hidden.label: general case 2 - exactly five characters after trim

solution.approach: For each test case, read the whole line as a string. Remove leading and trailing spaces (trim). The internal spaces are kept because they are part of the visible characters. The problem only cares about the number of characters, not their case, so converting to a single case is optional. After trimming, compute the length of the resulting string. If the length > 5 output "Long String", else output "Short String". The algorithm runs in O(total number of characters) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all input strings) – each character is examined at most a constant number of times.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the input buffer.
